title: Cyphocalyx aridus
taxonomy: Cyphocalyx aridus is a species of liverwort, which is a type of non-vascular plant. The liverworts belong to the division Marchantiophyta and are further classified into two main groups: the Jungermanniidae and the Marchantiidae. Cyphocalyx aridus specifically falls under the group Jungermanniidae, which includes more than 3,000 species of liverworts. This classification is based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and reproductive characteristics. The exact classification of Cyphocalyx aridus may also be further specified at the genus and species levels within the broader classification of liverworts.

intro: Cyphocalyx aridus, commonly known as the Australian rainforest fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Cyphocalyx aridus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cyphocalyx aridus is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or tea, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for various health conditions.
